summary: Ukrainian forces intensified UAV strikes on Russian logistical targets, hitting oil terminals, a tanker and a missile carrier, while Russia launched multiple Shahed attacks on Kyiv and surrounding regions. Both sides reported increased use of cruise missiles, including H‑101 launches from Tu‑95MS bombers, and preparations for a large‑scale Russian missile barrage dubbed “Oreshnyk”. Ukrainian intelligence also claimed a successful sabotage of the Russian chemical plant Metafrax Chemicals in the Perm region, halting its production.
key_points: Ukrainian drones struck the Russian oil terminal Sheshharris, the Grousova refinery, a shadow‑fleet tanker and a missile carrier, causing fires and significant material loss (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian Shahed drones massed over Kyiv from the north, with reports of up to 40 aircraft heading toward Obolon and Troieshchyna (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67041).
Russian Tu‑95MS bombers launched H‑101 cruise missiles, indicating a renewed long‑range strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/93279).
Ukrainian special forces reportedly sabotaged the Metafrax Chemicals plant in the Perm region, halting production for key war materiel (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of an imminent large‑scale Russian missile attack using the “Oreshnyk” system within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over northern oblasts (Chernihiv, Sumy, Poltava) heading toward Kyiv and frontline towns (Source: @kpszsu/62568, @kpszsu/62570, @kpszsu/62564).
Ukrainian forces reported advancing up to 800 m on several sectors of the Sumy front, with continued skirmishes in Zapselye and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian forces claimed capture of a Colombian mercenary urging Ukrainian troops to surrender (Source: @tass_agency/377232).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV strikes have crippled key Russian logistical nodes, including oil terminals and a tanker, while Russia intensified Shahed drone attacks on Kyiv and launched H‑101 cruise missiles from Tu‑95MS bombers. Both sides are preparing for a major missile exchange, with Ukraine warning of an imminent Russian “Oreshnyk” barrage and reporting modest territorial gains on the Sumy front. Sabotage operations have also hit Russian war‑industry assets deep inside the Russian Federation, notably the Metafrax Chemicals plant in Perm.

points: Ukrainian drones hit the Sheshharris oil terminal on the Black Sea, igniting fires and disrupting up to 75 million tonnes of annual oil throughput (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
A strike on the Grousova refinery near Novorossisk caused a blaze at a storage capacity of roughly 1.2 million tonnes of fuel (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying a shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS in the Black Sea, reducing Russian maritime logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian special operations sabotaged the Metafrax Chemicals plant in Perm, halting production of explosives, rocket engines and drone components (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
On the Sumy front, Ukrainian assault aircraft advanced up to 800 m on twenty sectors, with notable gains in Zapselye and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a large‑scale Russian missile barrage using the “Oreshnyk” system, potentially within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Ukrainian UAV groups were observed over Chernihiv and Sumy heading toward Kyiv, indicating coordinated air‑defense pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62568, @kpszsu/62570).
Ukrainian forces claimed capture of a Colombian mercenary urging surrender, used for propaganda purposes (Source: @tass_agency/377232).
Russian Tu‑95MS strategic bombers launched H‑101 cruise missiles, demonstrating restored long‑range strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/93279).
Massive Shahed drone waves targeted Kyiv, with up to 40 drones reported inbound from the north toward Obolon and Troieshchyna (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67041).
Additional Shahed formations were detected over Chernihiv, Sumy and Poltava, heading toward Kyiv and frontline towns (Source: @kpszsu/62568, @kpszsu/62570, @kpszsu/62564).
Russian forces reported repelling Ukrainian attacks on Moscow and Sevastopol, and claimed UAV interceptions in Kaluga and Rostov regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian air‑defence units engaged Ukrainian UAVs over the Donetsk region, reporting a new group heading north from Bilozerevskoye (Source: @kpszsu/62549).
Russian officials warned of the “Oreshnyk” missile system preparation, aligning with Ukrainian intelligence warnings (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Russian forces claimed a captured Colombian mercenary urging Ukrainian troops to surrender, likely a psychological‑operations effort (Source: @tass_agency/377232).
Russian logistics continued to rely on oil terminals such as Sheshharris, despite Ukrainian strikes, indicating resilience in fuel supply chains (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian drones attacked the oil terminal Sheshharris in the Black Sea region, disrupting Russian fuel export capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian forces reported advancing up to 800 m on the Sumy front across twenty sectors, with skirmishes in Zapselye and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian Shahed drones were observed over Sumy, with at least one attack on a funeral column causing civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).
Ukrainian UAV groups were noted heading from Chernihiv toward Slavutych and Snovsk, indicating pressure on eastern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62568).
Russian air‑defence claimed downing multiple UAVs over the Kaluga and Rostov regions, suggesting extended engagement zones (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian special forces sabotaged the Metafrax Chemicals plant in Perm, affecting eastern Russian war‑industry production (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
Russian forces reported repelling Ukrainian attacks on Moscow, indicating a broader eastern front engagement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian artillery and UAVs targeted fuel depots in the Donetsk area, with mixed reports of success (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Shahed drone swarms from the north targeted Kyiv, with up to 40 aircraft heading toward Obolon and Troieshchyna (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67041).
Ukrainian UAV groups were observed over Chernihiv moving toward Kyiv, indicating heightened aerial activity in the north (Source: @kpszsu/62568).
Russian air‑defence reported intercepting UAVs over Kaluga districts, showing defensive depth north of the front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian forces reported a new UAV group over Poltava heading toward Kyiv, suggesting coordinated northern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62570).
Russian strategic bombers launched H‑101 cruise missiles, likely targeting northern Ukrainian infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/93279).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of an imminent Russian missile barrage, potentially affecting northern cities (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Ukrainian drones were reported over Sumy, causing civilian casualties in a funeral column (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).
Russian forces claimed capture of a Colombian mercenary, possibly to influence northern morale (Source: @tass_agency/377232).
Ukrainian drones struck the Grousova refinery near Novorossisk, igniting a fire at a facility storing ~1.2 million tonnes of fuel (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying a shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS in the Black Sea, reducing Russian maritime logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian Shahed attacks were reported over Kherson region, with UAVs passing near Berislav toward Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/62552).
Ukrainian UAV groups targeted oil terminals in the Black Sea, including Sheshharris, affecting Russian southern supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian air‑defence claimed downing UAVs over Rostov region, indicating southern aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian special operations sabotaged the Metafrax Chemicals plant in Perm, indirectly affecting southern Russian war‑industry logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
Russian forces prepared the “Oreshnyk” missile system, potentially targeting southern Ukrainian cities (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Ukrainian artillery engagements reported near the Oskol River on the Kupyansk front, affecting southern operational depth (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian Tu‑95MS bombers launched H‑101 cruise missiles, marking a resurgence of long‑range strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/93279).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted multiple strikes on Russian oil infrastructure, including Sheshharris and Grousova, causing fires and operational disruption (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Shahed drone swarms from the north and east targeted Kyiv, with reports of up to 40 drones inbound (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67041).
Ukrainian forces reported downing 106 drones in a single day, indicating intense aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian air‑defence reported interceptions of UAVs over Kaluga, Rostov and other regions, showing extended defensive perimeter (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian sabotage of the Metafrax Chemicals plant in Perm halted production of key aerospace and missile components (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of an imminent Russian “Oreshnyk” missile barrage, potentially involving large‑scale ballistic or cruise missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Ukrainian UAV groups were observed over Chernihiv, Sumy and Poltava, indicating coordinated aerial pressure across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62568, @kpszsu/62570).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones destroyed the Sheshharris oil terminal on the Black Sea, igniting fires and disrupting up to 75 million tonnes of annual oil throughput (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian Shahed drone swarms massed over Kyiv, with up to 40 aircraft reported heading toward Obolon and Troieshchyna (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67041).
Russian Tu‑95MS bombers launched H‑101 cruise missiles, restoring long‑range strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/93279).
Ukrainian special forces sabotaged the Metafrax Chemicals plant in Perm, halting production of explosives and rocket engines (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of an imminent Russian missile barrage using the “Oreshnyk” system within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Ukrainian UAVs struck the Grousova refinery near Novorossisk, causing a fire at a facility storing ~1.2 million tonnes of fuel (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian forces reported advancing up to 800 m on the Sumy front across twenty sectors, with notable gains in Zapselye and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian forces claimed capture of a Colombian mercenary urging Ukrainian troops to surrender, likely a psychological‑operations move (Source: @tass_agency/377232).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia is escalating aerial pressure through massed Shahed drone attacks on Kyiv and renewed cruise‑missile launches, while preparing a large‑scale “Oreshnyk” missile barrage that could overwhelm Ukrainian air‑defences. Ukraine counters with intensified UAV strikes on high‑value Russian logistics, sabotage of deep‑strike industrial sites, and modest territorial gains on the Sumy front, indicating a shift toward precision strikes and disruption of Russian supply chains. The combined effect raises the risk of rapid escalation and heightened civilian vulnerability across major urban centers.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have increased diplomatic warnings, with the U.S. embassy alerting to a possible massive Russian air attack, while Ukraine seeks accelerated delivery of air‑defence systems. Russia’s propaganda efforts, including the capture of a foreign mercenary, aim to undermine Ukrainian morale. Regional actors remain on alert, reinforcing air‑defence assets around Kyiv and key southern ports to mitigate the anticipated missile barrage.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring confirms a surge in Shahed drone activity from northern oblasts, correlating with Russian strategic bomber sorties. Ukrainian sabotage of the Perm chemical plant suggests expanded special‑operations reach deep inside Russian territory. The “Oreshnyk” threat likely involves a mixed salvo of cruise and ballistic missiles, requiring layered defence. Continued UAV engagements indicate both sides are leveraging low‑cost aerial platforms to shape battlefield dynamics.
